items.name: Platform Mesh
items.description:
The Platform Mesh is the main Platform API for users and technical services to order and orchestrate capabilities attached to the environment.

Its guiding and design principle is inherited from Kubernetes’s declarative API approach with its digital twin manifests, the Kubernetes Resource Model (KRM). It utilizes and refines the upstream project KCP for its purpose.

items.name: Katalis
items.description:
Katalis should enable a cloud edge continuum in both directions:

The consumption of telco operator compute/storage resources from gardener controlled environments, i.E. to deploy application fragments close the edge of a UE to fulfill latency requirements
The extension of telco operator federation to software cloud providers resources

items.name: OpenBAO
items.description: OpenBao is an identity-based secrets and encryption management system. A secret is anything that you want to tightly control access to, such as API encryption keys, passwords, and certificates. OpenBao provides encryption services that are gated by authentication and authorization methods. Using OpenBao’s UI, CLI, or HTTP API, access to secrets and other sensitive data can be securely stored and managed, tightly controlled (restricted), and auditable.

items.name: Open Crypto Broker
items.description: The Open Crypto Broker allows to move cryptographic operations into a managed environments. This allows to enforce governance and policies as to which algorithms are allowed or not.

items.name: Open Key Chain Manager
items.description: OpenKCM serves as a central management layer for encryption of data at-rest. It comes with built-in multi-tenancy support and allows each tenant to stay in control of designing keychains and managing their lifecycle.

items.name: Open Component Model
items.description: The Open Component Model (OCM) is an open standard + reference implementation for describing software artifacts in a uniform and machine-readable way. OCM enables consistent end-to-end lifecycle management processes and a secure software supply chain.

items.name: Greenhouse
items.description: Greenhouse is a Kubernetes based day 2 operations platform focusing on providing a set of opinionated tools & operational processes for managing cloud native infrastructure.

items.name: Open Micro Frontend Platform
items.description: The Open Micro Frontend Platform (OpenMFP) aims to provide the opinionated, “batteries-included” platform for building portals and complex web-based applications with enterprise qualities.

items.name: Luigi
items.description: Luigi is a micro frontend framework that enables you to create a unified user experience across a large set of functionality.

items.name: Konfidence
items.description: Konfidence is an open-source software delivery framework. It ensures that only tested and approved versions reach production, addressing a common challenge in complex IT landscapes.

items.name: Open Resource Discovery
items.description: ORD is an open protocol for the decentralized publishing and discovery of application and service metadata. It provides a structured schema for metadata such as endpoints, capabilities, documentation links, and ownership details, ensuring that application resources like APIs, events, data products and AI agents can be discovered, understood, and integrated consistently across different systems and marketplaces.

items.name: United Metadata Service
items.description: The Unified Metadata Service (UMS) is a centralized platform designed to consolidate fragmented metadata, such as APIs, events, services, and tenant information across complex landscapes into a unified, extensible, and model-driven environment. UMS provides a unified metadata layer that enables marketplaces to offer consistent, searchable service catalogs and allows AI systems to interpret, discover, and orchestrate APIs, events and data products automatically.

items.name: Open Managed Control Plane
items.description:
OpenMCP is a managed Infrastructure-as-data orchestration layer, designed to streamline and automate the management of cloud resources and corresponding services.
It involves the coordination and management of various Infrastructure-as-data services, to support the automation of workflows such as provisioning and scaling of workloads.

items.name: Gardener
items.description: Deliver fully-managed clusters at scale everywhere with your own Gardener installation.

items.name: Garden Linux
items.description: Garden Linux is a Debian GNU/Linux derivate that aims to provide small, auditable Linux images for most cloud providers (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP etc.) and bare-metal machines.

items.name: IronCore
items.description: Next Generation Cloud Native Infrastructure as a Service and Bare Metal Automation. IronCore is a modular and scalable system designed to manage compute, networking, and storage resources efficiently.
